task:
ATOM 0 (Atom A) — Fail-fast preflight guard for a non-git primary root.

CONTEXT / DEFECT (confirmed run_174): launching a run against a primary root that is NOT a git repository crashes with a cryptic `git rev-parse HEAD` failure at run-start. The crash is in packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts at line ~601: `const trunkSha = await git.headSha(workspaceRepo)` (headSha runs `git rev-parse HEAD` via packages/core/src/commit-gate/git.ts:61-65). There is currently NO guard: CLI preflight (runner.ts ~579-588) only probes the persona CLIs, and the only repo guard after it is the detached-HEAD check (runner.ts ~600-607).

READ FIRST (owning files/tests): packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts (the preflight block ~579-588 and the launch block ~600-607); packages/core/src/commit-gate/git.ts (headSha/currentBranch, ~61-65, and the existing PreflightError / DirtyWorkingTreeError error types used nearby); packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts (~line 2373 'preflight failure aborts before spawning and marks the run failed' — mirror this test's structure for your new test). Look at how DirtyWorkingTreeError is constructed/thrown for detached HEAD and follow that exact pattern.

WHAT TO CHANGE: before the `git.headSha(workspaceRepo)` call at runner.ts ~601, add a fail-fast check that detects when `workspaceRepo` is not a git repository and refuses the launch with a CLEAR founder-facing message, e.g. 'primary root is not a git repository — initialize it first (run `git init`)'. Detect via a git query (e.g. `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` / `--git-dir`) added as a small method on the git module in commit-gate/git.ts (e.g. `isGitRepo(cwd): Promise<boolean>`), not by stat-ing `.git` directly (a worktree/submodule has no `.git` dir). On refusal: set run status to 'failed', record a run event (mirror the existing 'direct-mode-refused' event shape, e.g. reason 'not-a-git-repo'), and throw a clear error (reuse DirtyWorkingTreeError or add a focused error type consistent with the existing ones). ZERO run must start — the guard fires BEFORE any work/commit.

MUST NOT BREAK: existing git roots (with OR without a remote) launch exactly as before; detached-HEAD behavior unchanged; do not touch scaffold or workspace-create here (that is Atom B). A remote is NOT required and must NOT be added anywhere.

EXIT CRITERION / REQUIRED EVIDENCE: add a focused test in packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts proving that a launch against a non-git primary root surfaces the guarded founder-facing message at preflight with zero run started (status failed, the new event recorded, headSha never reached), AND that a normal git root still launches. Run the test and the package typecheck; report the exact command(s), pass/fail counts, and the new test name. Do not weaken or skip any existing test. Keep the diff tight: commit-gate/git.ts (new isGitRepo) + runner.ts (the guard) + runner.test.ts (the new test) only.

ATOM 1 (Atom B) — Scaffold initializes a LOCAL git repo on a non-git primary root so the workspace becomes committed and launch-ready.

CONTEXT / DEFECT (confirmed run_174): creating a workspace on a primary root that is NOT a git repo scaffolds the `cocoder/` zone but cannot commit it (`workspace-create` audits `governanceCommitted: false`, `governanceCommittedSha: null`), leaving the root unlaunchable. Atom 0 (already committed) added a fail-fast launch guard; THIS atom fixes the root cause so a fresh non-git root onboards cleanly. ONLY a LOCAL git repo is required — a remote is NEVER added (the commit spine only does add/commit/rev-parse and never pushes).

READ FIRST (owning files/tests): packages/daemon/src/routes.ts — `scaffoldWorkspaceGovernance(root)` (~354-361) and the POST /workspaces handler that calls it then `commitGovernance(...)` and audits `governanceCommitted` / `governanceCommittedSha` (~738-752). packages/core/src/commit-gate/git.ts — the Git interface and makeGit() (you ADD a local-init method here; reuse the existing `isGitRepo` from Atom 0 for the not-a-repo check — do NOT stat `.git`). packages/core/src/scaffold/scaffold.ts — `scaffoldCocoderZone` (~75-96, create-only semantics; COCODER_ZONE_ENTRIES / hasExistingRepoContent ~8,53-58). Tests: packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts — the existing git-root happy path (~2141-2178) and the existing NON-git test 'POST /workspaces succeeds and audits governance commit failure for a non-git primary root' (~2180-2201) which currently asserts `governanceCommittedSha` is null — that asserted behavior is what this atom intentionally CHANGES, so update that test to the new contract (now it commits). packages/core/tests/commit-gate.test.ts and scaffold.test.ts for unit coverage of the new init.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Add a method to the Git interface + makeGit() in commit-gate/git.ts to initialize a LOCAL repo, e.g. `initRepo(cwd): Promise<void>` running `git init -b main` (deterministic initial branch = main, matching the run's branch). It must NOT add any remote.
2. In the workspace-create flow (routes.ts, inside/around `scaffoldWorkspaceGovernance` and before `commitGovernance`): when `isGitRepo(primaryRoot)` is false, run `initRepo(primaryRoot)`, and create-only-seed a starter root `.gitignore` (only if absent — do not overwrite an existing one) ignoring at least `.DS_Store` and common binary/large-file patterns. Then let the existing `commitGovernance` commit the scaffolded `cocoder/` zone (+ the .gitignore) through the spine so the root is launch-ready with a real HEAD commit. Ensure the spine's first commit works with no pre-existing HEAD.
3. An EXISTING git root (with OR without a remote) must be COMPLETELY untouched — no init, no .gitignore injection, no remote changes; its current behavior is unchanged.

MUST NOT BREAK: the existing git-root workspace-create happy path; create-only scaffold semantics (never clobber founder files); Atom 0's launch guard (a freshly-initialized root must now PASS that guard and launch). Never add or require a remote anywhere.

EXIT CRITERION / REQUIRED EVIDENCE: update the existing non-git mutations.test.ts test (and/or add a focused one) proving that POST /workspaces on a FRESH non-git primary root now: returns `governanceCommitted: true` with a real non-null `governanceCommittedSha`; leaves a real local git repo (an initialized repo on branch `main` with a HEAD commit that contains the scaffolded `cocoder/` zone); configures NO remote; and seeds a root `.gitignore`. Add a separate assertion/test that an existing git root is untouched (no extra init/remote). Add commit-gate unit coverage for the new init method. Run the daemon test file and the core typecheck yourself and report exact command(s), pass/fail counts, and test names. Do not weaken or skip existing tests; keep the diff tight to git.ts, routes.ts (+ scaffold.ts only if the .gitignore seed naturally lives there), and the named tests.
